title: Dense per-patch field voting: 64 local place votes with learned fusion replace the global 1024-way template head
hypothesis: A read-only probe of the last kept model (berlin+prignitz, midday, matched random headings, 300 crops/condition) shows train-split crops localize to 267-400 m median while eval-split crops sit at 708-1153 m: a ~3x held-out-location generalization gap now dominates the metric, and it is structural, not a lighting problem. The current head is a single 560-d whole-crop descriptor feeding a 573k-param 1024-way FC -- it memorizes whole-crop gestalt templates, which cannot fire correctly on terrain whose pixels were never seen (eval blocks are fully excluded from training with a buffer). But the geometry helps: eval blocks are 360 px squares and crop windows are 182 px, so ~76% of eval crops contain SOME train-seen pixels at a known in-crop offset -- and a global-template head has no mechanism to exploit partially recognized content. Dense per-patch voting does exactly that: each of the 8x8 feature-grid cells casts its own full-map probability field for its OWN patch of ground, supervised with its own patch-level target; at inference a learned informativeness softmax weights the 64 per-patch log-fields into one fused field. Patches over recognized terrain vote precisely and carry the answer; unrecognizable patches are down-weighted. Because the patch-offset pattern is symmetric about the crop center, the fused field peaks at the crop center with no heading knowledge needed. Weight sharing (one 1x1 MLP serves all 64 positions) also cuts head capacity from ~573k to ~137k params, so per-location gestalt memorization -- the thing the probe shows failing to transfer -- is architecturally discouraged: local features must become location-predictive everywhere in the crop. This is materially different from reverted exp 4, which regressed per-patch COORDINATES (sigmoid + smooth-L1, mean-of-64 decode) on a from-scratch encoder with 4.8k crops: mean-decoding suffers the centroid-shrinkage disease exp 14 later diagnosed and fixed, and the from-scratch trunk had no local features worth reading. This design keeps every kept mechanism -- pretrained trunk (exp 11), calibrated Gaussian-CE field + beta-sharpened decode (exps 2/14), C4 rotation vote (exp 16), shape-based confidence + calibration (exp 15), three-realization renders (exp 17) -- and changes only who produces the field logits.
method: model/model.py: delete layout_squeeze, loc_logits, dark_logits and the luminance gate; add a shared per-position head (1x1 conv 48->128, ReLU, 1x1 conv 128->1024) producing per-patch field logits [*,1024,8,8] and a 1x1 conv 48->1 whose softmax over the 64 positions gives fusion weights; fused per-view logits = sum_p w_p * logits_p; everything downstream (C4 logit mean, sharpened decode, conf head, conf_shift calibration, ONNX export) is unchanged. model/train.py: store each training crop's heading angle next to its (u,v) target; the loss adds a dense term -- each patch's logits get Gaussian-CE against the map cell of that patch's own ground content, computed as crop center + R(heading) . patch offset, on the identity C4 view.
expected_outcome: Primary worst-case median error drops from 1054.87 m into the 650-900 m band. Mechanism-specific signature to check in the logs: the eval-vs-train gap narrows (probe baseline: berlin midday eval 1153 m vs train 400 m; prignitz 708 vs 267) and the improvement should appear across ALL lighting buckets, since the generalization gap is lighting-independent. Bounded downside as in every kept experiment: at init the per-patch fields are near-uniform, so the fused field is uniform and the sharpened decode starts at the map center. Exported model shrinks from 3.3 MB to ~1.3 MB, well inside the 4 MiB gate; latency stays trunk-dominated (~3 ms proxy).
init_strategy: pretrained:mobilenet_v3_small IMAGENET1K_V1 features[0..8] (torchvision, BSD-3) -- unchanged from exp 11
eli5: Until now the model looked at the whole photo at once and asked 'which spot in the city does this whole picture remind me of?' -- like recognizing a street from a single glance. Our measurement shows it does that three times better on places it studied than on the gaps in between, which is exactly where it gets tested. Now each of 64 small tiles of the photo votes independently -- 'my little piece of ground belongs THERE' -- and a learned referee counts trustworthy tiles more heavily before merging the votes. Even over unfamiliar ground, a few tiles usually catch a road or building the model does know, and those few good votes can carry the answer.

stages.name: Camera frame
stages.detail: 128×128 px aerial crop, one of 6 simulated lighting conditions

stages.name: Rotation fan
stages.detail: the crop is copied into its four 90° turns (exact pixel re-arrangements, no blur) and all four copies travel through the same network side by side

stages.name: Feature extractor
stages.detail: ImageNet-pretrained MobileNetV3-Small (first 9 blocks, 190k params), gently fine-tuned

stages.name: Patch voters
stages.detail: every one of the 8×8 feature-grid cells casts its own full-city probability vote for where ITS OWN little piece of ground sits — 64 independent local votes instead of one whole-picture guess (replaces the layout summary and lighting gate)

stages.name: Vote fusion
stages.detail: a tiny learned referee scores how informative each tile looks and blends the 64 votes into one heat map, trustworthy tiles counting more; the four turned copies are then averaged into one voted map as before

stages.name: Decode
stages.detail: the heat map's contrast is turned up first (peaks boosted, faint background suppressed), then the answer is the balance point of the sharpened map — committing to the strongest hotspot instead of averaging every faint guess across the whole city

stages.name: Confidence
stages.detail: the model inspects the shape of its own heat map — one crisp hotspot means sure, a washed-out smear means unsure — and below a tuned bar it says 'no fix' instead of guessing; the bar is set so it still answers at least 40% of the time in every lighting condition

stages.name: Training signal
stages.detail: every tile's vote is graded against the true location of that tile's own piece of ground (worked out exactly from the crop's center and rotation) on top of the usual grading of the final combined answer — 64 report cards per photo instead of one

stages.name: Training data
stages.detail: each practice image comes from one of three independently rolled versions of the lighting simulator — same city, same physics, but different random sensor static and a different random choice of which streetlights/windows are lit — so memorizing one roll's grain and lamp pattern no longer pays off

three-realization training renders (exp 17) — unchanged · calibration unchanged</text></svg>
implementation_brief:
GOAL: replace the global two-expert field head with a dense per-patch field-voting head, and add dense per-patch supervision. Touch ONLY model/model.py and model/train.py. Do not touch pipeline/ or anything in /FROZEN.

FIXED CONTRACTS (restate, must survive verbatim): (1) train.py CLI: `python -m model.train --area A --out-dir D [--data-dir P] [--epochs N] [--max-crops-per-bucket M] [--seed S]`, exporting one ONNX per area to <out-dir>/models/<area>.onnx, appending an info dict to <out-dir>/train_info.json and printing it as JSON. (2) ONNX export contract (model/model.py docstring): input name 'frame', shape 1x3x128x128 float32 in [0,1]; output [[u, v, conf]] with u,v normalized map coords, conf in [0,1]; opset 17, dynamo=False. Gates: file <= 4 MiB, host latency proxy <= 250 ms. (3) conf semantics: calibrated hit probability; the conf_shift buffer mechanism and calibrate_conf_shift flow in train.py stay exactly as they are. (4) Keep GRID_K=32, TARGET_SIGMA_CELLS=1.5, DECODE_BETA=3.0, GOOD_ERR_UV=0.05, _grid_centers, _c4_stack, _build_pretrained_trunk, IMAGENET_* buffers, export_onnx, estimate_position unchanged.

model/model.py changes:
1. Add constants: PATCH_N = 8 (trunk output is 8x8 at stride 16 for 128 px input), PATCH_STRIDE = 16, PATCH_MID_CH = 128, DENSE_W = 1.0.
2. In TinyLocNet.__init__: DELETE self.layout_squeeze, self.loc_logits, self.dark_logits, self.gate (and the layout_ch/gate_hidden ctor args). ADD: self.patch_mid = nn.Conv2d(feat_ch, PATCH_MID_CH, 1); self.patch_logits = nn.Conv2d(PATCH_MID_CH, grid_k*grid_k, 1); self.patch_w = nn.Conv2d(feat_ch, 1, 1). Keep self.conf_head (input dim feat_ch+3, unchanged), conf_shift, cell_u/cell_v buffers, norm buffers.
3. forward(x, return_logits=False): delete the lum computation and the gating. New flow: n = x.shape[0]; x4 = _c4_stack(x); fmap = self.features((x4 - norm_mean)/norm_std)  # [4N,48,8,8]; h = torch.relu(self.patch_mid(fmap)); pl = self.patch_logits(h)  # [4N,1024,8,8]; w = torch.softmax(self.patch_w(fmap).flatten(2), dim=2)  # [4N,1,64]; fused4 = (pl.flatten(2) * w).sum(dim=2)  # [4N,1024]; logits = fused4.reshape(4, n, -1).mean(dim=0)  # C4 vote, unchanged pattern; f = fmap.mean(dim=(2,3)).reshape(4, n, -1).mean(dim=0). Everything after (p = softmax(DECODE_BETA*logits), u, v, p1, u1, v1, peak, ent, gap, conf head with conf_shift, out = cat([u,v,conf])) is byte-for-byte identical to the current file. When return_logits is True, return (out, logits, pl[:n]) where pl[:n] is the identity view's per-patch logits [N, 1024, 8, 8] (rotation-major C4 stack order guarantees the first N entries are the un-rotated views). Default path returns out only, so ONNX export is untouched.
4. loss_fn: new signature loss_fn(pred, logits, patch_logits, target, map_wh) where target is [N,3] = (u, v, angle_deg) and map_wh = (width_px, height_px) floats for the area. Components: (a) fused CE: identical Gaussian-bump CE as now, using target[:, :2]. (b) coord L2: ((pred[:, :2] - target[:, :2])**2).sum(1).mean(), unchanged. (c) conf BCE: unchanged, using target[:, :2]. (d) NEW dense per-patch CE, weight DENSE_W: build crop-frame patch-center offsets in px: for row i, col j in 0..7: dx_c = (j+0.5)*PATCH_STRIDE - 64, dy_c = (i+0.5)*PATCH_STRIDE - 64; flat patch index p = i*8 + j (this matches .flatten(2) / [N,1024,8,8] row-major order). Map-frame offset for angle theta (radians, from angle_deg): dx_m = cos(theta)*dx_c - sin(theta)*dy_c; dy_m = sin(theta)*dx_c + cos(theta)*dy_c. This sign convention has been VERIFIED empirically against pipeline.dataset.extract_crop at angles 30/137/285 deg (marker test: img = np.zeros((400,400,3), np.uint8); img[240, 210] = 255, i.e. map offset (dx=+10, dy=+40) from center (200,200); c = extract_crop(img, 200, 200, ang); py, px = np.unravel_index(c[:,:,0].argmax(), c.shape[:2]); crop offset (px-64, py-64) maps back to ~(10, 40) under the matrix above). Re-run that snippet as a sanity check but do not change the matrix. Then per-patch target: u_p = clamp(target_u + dx_m/width_px, 0, 1), v_p = clamp(target_v + dy_m/height_px, 0, 1)  # [N,64] each. Build Gaussian cell targets exactly like the fused CE but batched over patches: g [N,64,1024], normalized over the cell dim; dense_ce = -(g * log_softmax(patch_logits.flatten(2).transpose(1,2), dim=2)).sum(2).mean(). Total: ce + DENSE_W*dense_ce + coord_loss + 0.3*conf_loss. Angles enter only through the dense term; keep everything torch-vectorized (precompute the 64 (dx_c, dy_c) as a buffer or module-level tensor).
5. Update the model.py docstring: append a paragraph 'Exp 18' explaining the dense per-patch voting head (probe evidence: train 267-400 m vs eval 708-1153 m median at matched headings; per-patch fields + learned informativeness softmax + weighted log-space fusion; head params drop 573k -> ~137k). Keep the ONNX contract sentence intact.

model/train.py changes:
1. load_training_tensors: record the rotation angle used for each crop: ys.append((*crop_center_norm(meta, c['cx'], c['cy']), angle)) so y is [N,3] float32 (angle in DEGREES, the same value passed to extract_crop). Also return meta (or its width/height) so the train loop can pass map_wh: simplest is `return x, y, meta`.
2. train_area: unpack x, y, meta; in the minibatch loop call out, logits, pl = model(xb, return_logits=True); loss = loss_fn(out, logits, pl, yb, (float(meta['width']), float(meta['height']))). Optimizer param groups unchanged (trunk 1e-4 / everything-else 1e-3; the new convs fall into the head group automatically). calibrate_conf_shift, ONNX export, info-dict flow all unchanged; update the info dict's descriptive fields only if trivial (e.g. add 'head': 'dense-per-patch 1x1 MLP, 64-vote fusion').
3. Do NOT change TRAIN_REALIZATIONS, CAL_CROPS_PER_BUCKET, MIN_KEEP_RATE, SCORER_CONF_THRESHOLD, epochs/batch defaults, or the uint8 tensor strategy. Update train.py's module docstring with a short Exp 18 note.

SANITY before finishing: (a) run the rotation-sign snippet above; (b) instantiate the model, forward a random 2x3x128x128 batch with return_logits=True, check shapes ([2,3], [2,1024], [2,1024,8,8]) and that loss_fn returns a finite scalar with a random [2,3] target (angles e.g. 37, 213) and map_wh (7000, 7000); (c) export ONNX and confirm file size <= 4 MiB (expect ~1.3 MB) and onnxruntime output shape [[u,v,conf]].
